query.sqlServer: SELECT d.name AS [Database], d.recovery_model_desc AS [Recovery Model], MAX(b.backup_finish_date) AS [Last Full Backup], DATEDIFF(HOUR,MAX(b.backup_finish_date),GETDATE()) AS [Hours Ago], CAST(MAX(b.backup_size)/1024.0/1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Size MB] FROM sys.databases d LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset b ON b.database_name = d.name AND b.type = 'D' WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0 GROUP BY d.name,d.recovery_model_desc ORDER BY [Last Full Backup] ASC

panels.title: Recent Backup History
panels.description: Last 50 backup operations from msdb, newest first.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 50 bs.database_name AS [Database], CASE bs.type WHEN 'D' THEN 'Full' WHEN 'I' THEN 'Differential' WHEN 'L' THEN 'Log' ELSE bs.type END AS [Type], bs.backup_start_date AS [Started], bs.backup_finish_date AS [Finished], DATEDIFF(MINUTE,bs.backup_start_date,bs.backup_finish_date) AS [Duration (min)], CAST(bs.backup_size/1024.0/1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Size MB], bs.user_name AS [By] FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs ORDER BY bs.backup_finish_date DESC

panels.title: Plan Cache Hit Ratio
panels.description: SQL Server plan cache hit ratio from sys.dm_os_performance_counters.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(cntr_value AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE counter_name = 'Cache Hit Ratio' AND object_name LIKE '%Plan Cache%' AND instance_name = '_Total'

panels.title: Plans Cached
panels.description: Total compiled plans currently in the SQL Server plan cache.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_exec_cached_plans WHERE cacheobjtype = 'Compiled Plan'

panels.title: Top CPU Queries
panels.description: Top 20 queries by total CPU time since last restart from sys.dm_exec_query_stats.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT TOP 20 CAST(qs.total_worker_time/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Total CPU (ms)], qs.execution_count AS [Executions], CAST(qs.total_worker_time/qs.execution_count/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Avg CPU (ms)], CAST(qs.total_logical_reads/qs.execution_count AS BIGINT) AS [Avg Reads], qs.last_execution_time AS [Last Exec], LEFT(REPLACE(REPLACE(st.text,CHAR(13),' '),CHAR(10),' '),150) AS [SQL Text] 
,qp.query_plan [Plan]
FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(qs.sql_handle) st 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_query_plan(qs.plan_handle) AS qp
ORDER BY qs.total_worker_time DESC

panels.title: Top I/O Queries
panels.description: Top 20 queries by total logical reads since last restart.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT TOP 20 CAST(qs.total_logical_reads/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Total Reads (K)], qs.execution_count AS [Executions], CAST(qs.total_logical_reads/qs.execution_count AS BIGINT) AS [Avg Reads], CAST(qs.total_worker_time/qs.execution_count/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Avg CPU (ms)], qs.last_execution_time AS [Last Exec], LEFT(REPLACE(REPLACE(st.text,CHAR(13),' '),CHAR(10),' '),150) AS [SQL Text] 
,qp.query_plan [Plan]
FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(qs.sql_handle) st 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_query_plan(qs.plan_handle) AS qp
ORDER BY qs.total_logical_reads DESC

panels.title: Top Duration Queries
panels.description: Top 20 queries by total elapsed time since last restart.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT TOP 20 CAST(qs.total_elapsed_time/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Total Duration (ms)], qs.execution_count AS [Executions], CAST(qs.total_elapsed_time/qs.execution_count/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Avg Duration (ms)], CAST(qs.total_logical_reads/qs.execution_count AS BIGINT) AS [Avg Reads], qs.last_execution_time AS [Last Exec], LEFT(REPLACE(REPLACE(st.text,CHAR(13),' '),CHAR(10),' '),150) AS [SQL Text] 
,qp.query_plan [Plan]
FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(qs.sql_handle) st 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_query_plan(qs.plan_handle) AS qp
ORDER BY qs.total_elapsed_time DESC

panels.title: Currently Running Jobs
panels.description: Number of SQL Agent jobs currently executing.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity ja JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j ON ja.job_id = j.job_id WHERE ja.session_id = (SELECT MAX(session_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity) AND ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL

panels.title: Failed Jobs (24h)
panels.description: SQL Agent job failures in the last 24 hours.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h WHERE h.run_status = 0 AND msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date,h.run_time) >= DATEADD(HOUR,-24,GETDATE()) AND h.step_id = 0

panels.title: Running Jobs
panels.description: Currently executing SQL Agent jobs with start time and duration.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job], ja.start_execution_date AS [Started], DATEDIFF(MINUTE,ja.start_execution_date,GETDATE()) AS [Running (min)], js.step_name AS [Current Step] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity ja JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j ON ja.job_id = j.job_id LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobsteps js ON ja.job_id = js.job_id AND ja.last_executed_step_id = js.step_id WHERE ja.session_id = (SELECT MAX(session_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity) AND ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL ORDER BY ja.start_execution_date

panels.title: Job Failures (Last 24 Hours)
panels.description: SQL Agent jobs that failed in the last 24 hours with error messages.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 50 j.name AS [Job], msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date,h.run_time) AS [Failed At], h.step_name AS [Step], h.message AS [Error] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j ON h.job_id = j.job_id WHERE h.run_status = 0 AND msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date,h.run_time) >= DATEADD(HOUR,-24,GETDATE()) ORDER BY msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date,h.run_time) DESC

panels.title: All Jobs Status
panels.description: All SQL Agent jobs with enabled status and last run outcome.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job], j.enabled AS [Enabled], CASE jh.run_status WHEN 0 THEN 'Failed' WHEN 1 THEN 'Succeeded' WHEN 2 THEN 'Retry' WHEN 3 THEN 'Cancelled' WHEN 4 THEN 'Running' ELSE 'Unknown' END AS [Last Status], msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(jh.run_date,jh.run_time) AS [Last Run] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory jh ON j.job_id = jh.job_id AND jh.instance_id = (SELECT MAX(instance_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h2 WHERE h2.job_id = j.job_id AND h2.step_id = 0) ORDER BY j.name

panels.title: TempDB Used (MB)
panels.description: Total TempDB space used by user objects, internal objects and version store.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(SUM(user_object_reserved_page_count+internal_object_reserved_page_count+version_store_reserved_page_count)*8/1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_db_file_space_usage

panels.title: Version Store (MB)
panels.description: TempDB space consumed by the version store. High values may indicate long-running transactions with RCSI/SI.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(SUM(version_store_reserved_page_count)*8/1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_db_file_space_usage

panels.title: Long Transactions (>5 min)
panels.description: Transactions open for more than 5 minutes. These can block version store cleanup.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_tran_active_transactions t JOIN sys.dm_tran_session_transactions st ON t.transaction_id = st.transaction_id WHERE DATEDIFF(MINUTE,t.transaction_begin_time,GETDATE()) > 5

panels.title: TempDB File Usage
panels.description: Per-file allocation, used and free space in TempDB.

query.sqlServer: SELECT f.name AS [File], f.physical_name AS [Path], CAST(f.size*8.0/1024 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Size MB], CAST((fs.user_object_reserved_page_count+fs.internal_object_reserved_page_count+fs.version_store_reserved_page_count)*8.0/1024 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Used MB], CAST(fs.unallocated_extent_page_count*8.0/1024 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Free MB] FROM tempdb.sys.dm_db_file_space_usage fs CROSS JOIN tempdb.sys.database_files f WHERE f.file_id = fs.file_id

panels.title: Top TempDB Users
panels.description: Sessions currently consuming the most TempDB space via user objects and internal objects.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 20 s.session_id AS [Session], s.login_name AS [Login], s.host_name AS [Host], s.program_name AS [Program], CAST((tsu.user_objects_alloc_page_count+tsu.internal_objects_alloc_page_count)*8.0/1024 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Alloc MB], CAST((tsu.user_objects_dealloc_page_count+tsu.internal_objects_dealloc_page_count)*8.0/1024 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Freed MB] FROM sys.dm_db_task_space_usage tsu JOIN sys.dm_exec_sessions s ON tsu.session_id = s.session_id WHERE tsu.user_objects_alloc_page_count+tsu.internal_objects_alloc_page_count > 0 ORDER BY tsu.user_objects_alloc_page_count+tsu.internal_objects_alloc_page_count DESC

panels.title: Long-Running Transactions
panels.description: Active transactions running for more than 5 minutes with session and login details.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 20 st.session_id AS [Session], t.transaction_begin_time AS [Started], DATEDIFF(MINUTE,t.transaction_begin_time,GETDATE()) AS [Duration (min)], t.transaction_type AS [Type], s.login_name AS [Login], s.host_name AS [Host], s.program_name AS [Program] FROM sys.dm_tran_active_transactions t JOIN sys.dm_tran_session_transactions st ON t.transaction_id = st.transaction_id JOIN sys.dm_exec_sessions s ON st.session_id = s.session_id WHERE DATEDIFF(MINUTE,t.transaction_begin_time,GETDATE()) > 5 ORDER BY t.transaction_begin_time ASC

SELECT CAST([value] AS INT) AS [Value]
FROM sys.configurations WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE name = 'max degree of parallelism'

SELECT SUM(work_queue_count) AS [Value]
FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE parent_node_id < 64 AND scheduler_id < 255

SELECT CASE WHEN sql_memory_model = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS [Value]
FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info WITH (NOLOCK)

SELECT COUNT(*) AS [Value]
FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE event_type IN (1, 2, 3)

SELECT COUNT(*) AS [Value]
FROM sys.sql_logins WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE is_policy_checked = 0 OR is_expiration_checked = 0

panels.title: Max Server Memory vs System RAM

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    sc.[value]                                      AS [configured_max_mem_MB],
    dosi.physical_memory_kb / 1024                  AS [total_system_mem_MB],
    CAST(sc.[value] * 100.0
         / NULLIF(dosi.physical_memory_kb / 1024, 0)
         AS DECIMAL(5,2))                           AS [pct_of_system_mem],
    CASE
        WHEN sc.[value] = 2147483647
            THEN '[WARNING] MaxServerMemory is default — revise before memory pressure occurs'
        WHEN sc.[value] > dosi.physical_memory_kb / 1024
            THEN '[WARNING] MaxServerMemory exceeds total physical RAM'
        WHEN sc.[value] * 100.0 / NULLIF(dosi.physical_memory_kb / 1024, 0) > 90
            THEN '[CAUTION] MaxServerMemory leaves <10 % for OS — consider reducing'
        ELSE '[OK]'
    END                                             AS [recommendation]
FROM sys.configurations sc WITH (NOLOCK)
CROSS JOIN sys.dm_os_sys_info dosi WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE sc.name = 'max server memory (MB)'

SELECT
    DB_NAME(f.database_id)                                          AS [database_name],
    f.name                                                          AS [logical_file],
    f.type_desc                                                     AS [file_type],
    fs.num_of_reads                                                 AS [read_count],
    fs.num_of_writes                                                AS [write_count],
    CAST(fs.io_stall_read_ms  / (1.0 + fs.num_of_reads)  AS NUMERIC(10,2)) AS [avg_read_latency_ms],
    CAST(fs.io_stall_write_ms / (1.0 + fs.num_of_writes) AS NUMERIC(10,2)) AS [avg_write_latency_ms],
    fs.io_stall                                                     AS [total_stall_ms],
    CASE
        WHEN fs.io_stall_read_ms  / (1.0 + fs.num_of_reads)  >= 50
          OR fs.io_stall_write_ms / (1.0 + fs.num_of_writes) >= 50
            THEN '[WARNING] High latency — review storage configuration'
        WHEN fs.io_stall_read_ms  / (1.0 + fs.num_of_reads)  >= 20
          OR fs.io_stall_write_ms / (1.0 + fs.num_of_writes) >= 20
            THEN '[CAUTION] Elevated latency'
        ELSE '[OK]'
    END                                                             AS [status]
FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL) AS fs
INNER JOIN sys.master_files AS f WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON fs.database_id = f.database_id AND fs.file_id = f.file_id
ORDER BY fs.io_stall DESC

panels.title: Top Wait Stats by Category (cumulative since restart)

query.sqlServer:
;WITH Waits AS (
    SELECT
        wait_type,
        wait_time_ms / 1000.0                                                           AS wait_time_s,
        signal_wait_time_ms / 1000.0                                                    AS signal_wait_time_s,
        (wait_time_ms - signal_wait_time_ms) / 1000.0                                  AS resource_wait_time_s,
        100.0 * wait_time_ms / NULLIF(SUM(wait_time_ms) OVER (), 0)                   AS pct,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY wait_time_ms DESC)                                  AS rn
    FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WITH (NOLOCK)
    WHERE wait_type NOT IN (
        'RESOURCE_QUEUE','SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP','SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',
        'SOSHOST_SLEEP','QDS_PERSIST_TASK_MAIN_LOOP_SLEEP','QDS_CLEANUP_STALE_QUERIES_TASK_MAIN_LOOP_SLEEP',
        'LOGMGR_QUEUE','CHECKPOINT_QUEUE','REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH','XE_TIMER_EVENT',
        'BROKER_TASK_STOP','CLR_MANUAL_EVENT','CLR_AUTO_EVENT','DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',
        'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT','BROKER_TO_FLUSH','XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT','XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN',
        'MSQL_XP','WAIT_FOR_RESULTS','CLR_SEMAPHORE','LAZYWRITER_SLEEP','SLEEP_TASK',
        'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK','SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH','WAITFOR','BROKER_EVENTHANDLER','TRACEWRITE',
        'FT_IFTSHC_MUTEX','BROKER_RECEIVE_WAITFOR','ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE','DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',
        'DBMIRRORING_CMD','BROKER_TRANSMITTER','SQLTRACE_WAIT_ENTRIES','SLEEP_BPOOL_FLUSH',
        'SQLTRACE_LOCK','DIRTY_PAGE_POLL','HADR_FILESTREAM_IOMGR_IOCOMPLETION',
        'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG'
    )
    AND wait_type NOT LIKE 'SLEEP_%'
)
SELECT
    W1.wait_type,
    CAST(W1.wait_time_s        AS DECIMAL(14,2)) AS wait_time_s,
    CAST(W1.signal_wait_time_s AS DECIMAL(14,2)) AS signal_wait_time_s,
    CAST(W1.pct                AS DECIMAL(14,2)) AS pct,
    CASE
        WHEN W1.wait_type = 'SOS_SCHEDULER_YIELD'                              THEN 'CPU'
        WHEN W1.wait_type = 'THREADPOOL'                                       THEN 'CPU - Worker Threads'
        WHEN W1.wait_type IN ('CXPACKET','EXCHANGE','CXCONSUMER',
             'HTBUILD','HTDELETE','HTMEMO','HTREINIT','HTREPARTITION')         THEN 'CPU - Parallelism'
        WHEN W1.wait_type LIKE 'LCK_%' OR W1.wait_type = 'LOCK'               THEN 'Lock'
        WHEN W1.wait_type LIKE 'LATCH_%'                                       THEN 'Latch'
        WHEN W1.wait_type LIKE 'PAGELATCH_%'                                   THEN 'Buffer Latch'
        WHEN W1.wait_type LIKE 'PAGEIOLATCH_%'                                 THEN 'Buffer IO'
        WHEN W1.wait_type IN ('LOGMGR','LOGBUFFER','LOGMGR_FLUSH','WRITELOG') THEN 'Tran Log IO'
        WHEN W1.wait_type IN ('ASYNC_NETWORK_IO','NET_WAITFOR_PACKET')         THEN 'Network IO'
        WHEN W1.wait_type LIKE 'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE_%'                          THEN 'Memory - Compilation'
        WHEN W1.wait_type IN ('UTIL_PAGE_ALLOC','SOS_VIRTUALMEMORY_LOW',
             'SOS_RESERVEDMEMBLOCKLIST','RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE','CMEMTHREAD')     THEN 'Memory'
        ELSE 'Other'
    END AS wait_category
FROM Waits W1
WHERE W1.wait_time_s >= 0.01
ORDER BY W1.rn

SELECT
    SUM(current_tasks_count)   AS [current_tasks],
    SUM(runnable_tasks_count)  AS [runnable_tasks],
    SUM(work_queue_count)      AS [work_queue_count],
    SUM(pending_disk_io_count) AS [pending_disk_io],
    CASE
        WHEN SUM(work_queue_count) > 1
            THEN '[WARNING] Work queue > 1 — possible worker thread exhaustion'
        WHEN AVG(runnable_tasks_count) > 2
            THEN '[CAUTION] High runnable task count — CPU pressure'
        ELSE '[OK]'
    END AS [status]
FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE parent_node_id < 64 AND scheduler_id < 255

SELECT
    DB_NAME(i.database_id)                                                          AS [database_name],
    OBJECT_NAME(i.object_id, i.database_id)                                         AS [table_name],
    i.equality_columns                                                              AS [equality_key_cols],
    i.inequality_columns                                                            AS [inequality_key_cols],
    i.included_columns                                                              AS [included_cols],
    s.user_seeks + s.user_scans                                                     AS [user_hits],
    CAST(s.avg_user_impact       AS DECIMAL(6,2))                                   AS [avg_impact_pct],
    CAST((s.user_seeks + s.user_scans)
         * s.avg_total_user_cost * s.avg_user_impact AS DECIMAL(18,0))              AS [score]
FROM sys.dm_db_missing_index_details i WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_groups g WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON i.index_handle = g.index_handle
INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_group_stats s WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON s.group_handle = g.index_group_handle
WHERE i.database_id > 4
  AND (s.user_seeks + s.user_scans) * s.avg_total_user_cost * s.avg_user_impact >= 100000
ORDER BY score DESC

SELECT
    name                    AS [login_name],
    is_policy_checked       AS [policy_checked],
    is_expiration_checked   AS [expiration_checked],
    LOGINPROPERTY(name, 'IsLocked')   AS [is_locked],
    LOGINPROPERTY(name, 'IsExpired')  AS [is_expired]
FROM sys.sql_logins WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE is_policy_checked = 0 OR is_expiration_checked = 0
ORDER BY name

SELECT
    DB_NAME(database_id) AS [database_name],
    file_id              AS [file_id],
    page_id              AS [page_id],
    CASE event_type
        WHEN 1 THEN 'Error 823 / unspecified 824'
        WHEN 2 THEN 'Bad Checksum'
        WHEN 3 THEN 'Torn Page'
        ELSE CAST(event_type AS VARCHAR(10))
    END                  AS [event_type],
    error_count          AS [error_count],
    last_update_date     AS [last_update_date]
FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE event_type IN (1, 2, 3)
ORDER BY DB_NAME(database_id), last_update_date DESC, file_id, page_id

dashboards.title: Replication Monitor
dashboards.description: Transactional and merge replication health, latency, and undistributed commands.

panels.title: Publications
panels.description: All publications configured on this server.

query.sqlServer: SELECT p.name AS [Publication], DB_NAME(p.database_id) AS [Database], CASE p.publication_type WHEN 0 THEN 'Transactional' WHEN 1 THEN 'Snapshot' WHEN 2 THEN 'Merge' ELSE CAST(p.publication_type AS VARCHAR(5)) END AS [Type], CASE p.status WHEN 0 THEN 'Inactive' WHEN 1 THEN 'Active' ELSE CAST(p.status AS VARCHAR(5)) END AS [Status], p.immediate_sync AS [ImmediateSync], p.allow_push AS [AllowPush], p.allow_pull AS [AllowPull] FROM distribution.dbo.MSpublications p WITH (NOLOCK) ORDER BY p.name

panels.title: Subscriptions
panels.description: Subscription status across all publications.

query.sqlServer: SELECT p.name AS [Publication], s.subscriber_db AS [SubscriberDB], ss.name AS [Subscriber], CASE s.status WHEN 0 THEN 'Inactive' WHEN 1 THEN 'Subscribed' WHEN 2 THEN 'Active' ELSE CAST(s.status AS VARCHAR(5)) END AS [Status], CASE s.subscription_type WHEN 0 THEN 'Push' WHEN 1 THEN 'Pull' ELSE '?' END AS [Type], s.sync_type AS [SyncType] FROM distribution.dbo.MSsubscriptions s WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN distribution.dbo.MSpublications p WITH (NOLOCK) ON s.publication_id = p.publication_id INNER JOIN sys.servers ss WITH (NOLOCK) ON s.subscriber_id = ss.server_id ORDER BY p.name, ss.name

panels.title: Undistributed Commands
panels.description: Commands waiting in the distribution database per subscription. High values indicate latency.

query.sqlServer: SELECT p.name AS [Publication], a.publisher_db AS [PublisherDB], ss.name AS [Subscriber], a.subscriber_db AS [SubscriberDB], SUM(CASE WHEN xact_seqno > delivered_seqno THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS [Undistributed], MAX(a.entry_time) AS [OldestEntry] FROM distribution.dbo.MSdistribution_status a WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN distribution.dbo.MSsubscriptions s WITH (NOLOCK) ON a.agent_id = s.agent_id INNER JOIN distribution.dbo.MSpublications p WITH (NOLOCK) ON s.publication_id = p.publication_id INNER JOIN sys.servers ss WITH (NOLOCK) ON s.subscriber_id = ss.server_id GROUP BY p.name, a.publisher_db, ss.name, a.subscriber_db ORDER BY [Undistributed] DESC

panels.title: Replication Agent History
panels.description: Recent log reader and distribution agent activity.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 50 a.name AS [Agent], CASE a.agent_type WHEN 1 THEN 'Snapshot' WHEN 2 THEN 'Log Reader' WHEN 3 THEN 'Distribution' WHEN 4 THEN 'Merge' ELSE CAST(a.agent_type AS VARCHAR(5)) END AS [Type], h.time AS [Time], h.comments AS [Message], h.duration AS [Duration(s)], h.delivered_commands AS [Commands], h.delivered_transactions AS [Transactions], CASE h.runstatus WHEN 1 THEN 'Start' WHEN 2 THEN 'Succeed' WHEN 3 THEN 'In Progress' WHEN 4 THEN 'Idle' WHEN 5 THEN 'Retry' WHEN 6 THEN 'Fail' ELSE CAST(h.runstatus AS VARCHAR(5)) END AS [Status] FROM distribution.dbo.MSdistribution_history h WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN distribution.dbo.MSdistribution_agents a WITH (NOLOCK) ON h.agent_id = a.id ORDER BY h.time DESC

dashboards.title: Always On Availability Groups
dashboards.description: Availability group health, replica sync status, redo queue, send queue, and failover readiness.

panels.title: AG Health Summary
panels.description: Overall health state of each availability group.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ag.name AS [AG Name], ags.primary_replica AS [Primary], CASE ags.primary_recovery_health_desc WHEN 'ONLINE' THEN 'Healthy' ELSE ISNULL(ags.primary_recovery_health_desc, 'N/A') END AS [PrimaryHealth], ags.synchronization_health_desc AS [SyncHealth], CASE ags.synchronization_health WHEN 0 THEN 'Not Healthy' WHEN 1 THEN 'Partially Healthy' WHEN 2 THEN 'Healthy' END AS [OverallHealth] FROM sys.availability_groups ag WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.dm_hadr_availability_group_states ags WITH (NOLOCK) ON ag.group_id = ags.group_id ORDER BY ag.name

panels.title: Replica Status
panels.description: Per-replica availability mode, failover mode, connection state, and role.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ag.name AS [AG Name], ar.replica_server_name AS [Replica], ars.role_desc AS [Role], ar.availability_mode_desc AS [AvailabilityMode], ar.failover_mode_desc AS [FailoverMode], ars.connected_state_desc AS [ConnectionState], ars.synchronization_health_desc AS [SyncHealth], ars.operational_state_desc AS [OperationalState], ars.last_connect_error_number AS [LastErrorNo], ars.last_connect_error_description AS [LastError] FROM sys.availability_replicas ar WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.availability_groups ag WITH (NOLOCK) ON ar.group_id = ag.group_id INNER JOIN sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states ars WITH (NOLOCK) ON ar.replica_id = ars.replica_id ORDER BY ag.name, ars.role_desc DESC, ar.replica_server_name

panels.title: Database Sync Status
panels.description: Per-database synchronization state including redo queue, send queue, and log rates.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ag.name AS [AG Name], ar.replica_server_name AS [Replica], DB_NAME(drs.database_id) AS [Database], drs.synchronization_state_desc AS [SyncState], drs.synchronization_health_desc AS [SyncHealth], drs.is_suspended AS [Suspended], CAST(drs.log_send_queue_size AS BIGINT) AS [SendQueue(KB)], CAST(drs.redo_queue_size AS BIGINT) AS [RedoQueue(KB)], CAST(drs.log_send_rate AS BIGINT) AS [SendRate(KB/s)], CAST(drs.redo_rate AS BIGINT) AS [RedoRate(KB/s)], drs.last_sent_time AS [LastSent], drs.last_received_time AS [LastReceived], drs.last_hardened_time AS [LastHardened], drs.last_redone_time AS [LastRedone] FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states drs WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.availability_replicas ar WITH (NOLOCK) ON drs.replica_id = ar.replica_id INNER JOIN sys.availability_groups ag WITH (NOLOCK) ON ar.group_id = ag.group_id ORDER BY ag.name, ar.replica_server_name, DB_NAME(drs.database_id)

panels.title: Send Queue Trend
panels.description: Log send queue size over time across all AG databases. Rising trends indicate network or throughput issues.

query.sqlServer: SELECT GETDATE() AS [Time], ar.replica_server_name + ' / ' + DB_NAME(drs.database_id) AS [Series], CAST(drs.log_send_queue_size AS BIGINT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states drs WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.availability_replicas ar WITH (NOLOCK) ON drs.replica_id = ar.replica_id WHERE drs.is_local = 0 ORDER BY [Value] DESC

panels.title: Redo Queue Trend
panels.description: Redo queue size over time. High values mean the secondary is falling behind.

query.sqlServer: SELECT GETDATE() AS [Time], ar.replica_server_name + ' / ' + DB_NAME(drs.database_id) AS [Series], CAST(drs.redo_queue_size AS BIGINT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states drs WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.availability_replicas ar WITH (NOLOCK) ON drs.replica_id = ar.replica_id WHERE drs.is_local = 0 ORDER BY [Value] DESC

panels.title: AG Listeners
panels.description: Availability group listener endpoints and IP configuration.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ag.name AS [AG Name], l.dns_name AS [Listener], l.port AS [Port], lip.ip_address AS [IP Address], lip.ip_subnet_mask AS [Subnet], lip.state_desc AS [State] FROM sys.availability_group_listeners l WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.availability_groups ag WITH (NOLOCK) ON l.group_id = ag.group_id INNER JOIN sys.availability_group_listener_ip_addresses lip WITH (NOLOCK) ON l.listener_id = lip.listener_id ORDER BY ag.name, l.dns_name

dashboards.title: SQL Agent Job Monitor
dashboards.description: SQL Server Agent job status, history, schedules, and failed job details.

panels.title: Running Jobs
panels.description: Currently executing SQL Agent jobs.

query.sqlServer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS [Value] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity ja WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) ON ja.job_id = j.job_id WHERE ja.session_id = (SELECT MAX(session_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity WITH (NOLOCK)) AND ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL

panels.title: Failed (24h)
panels.description: Jobs that failed in the last 24 hours.

query.sqlServer: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT j.name) AS [Value] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) ON h.job_id = j.job_id WHERE h.step_id = 0 AND h.run_status = 0 AND msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time) >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE())

panels.title: Succeeded (24h)
panels.description: Jobs that completed successfully in the last 24 hours.

query.sqlServer: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT j.name) AS [Value] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) ON h.job_id = j.job_id WHERE h.step_id = 0 AND h.run_status = 1 AND msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time) >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE())

panels.title: Disabled Jobs
panels.description: Total number of disabled agent jobs.

query.sqlServer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS [Value] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs WITH (NOLOCK) WHERE enabled = 0

panels.title: All Jobs — Last Outcome
panels.description: Every SQL Agent job with its last run status, duration, and next scheduled run.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job Name], CASE j.enabled WHEN 1 THEN 'Enabled' ELSE 'Disabled' END AS [Status], CASE WHEN ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL THEN 'Running' WHEN h.run_status = 0 THEN 'Failed' WHEN h.run_status = 1 THEN 'Succeeded' WHEN h.run_status = 2 THEN 'Retry' WHEN h.run_status = 3 THEN 'Cancelled' ELSE 'Unknown' END AS [LastOutcome], CASE WHEN ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL THEN CONVERT(VARCHAR, ja.start_execution_date, 120) WHEN h.run_date IS NOT NULL THEN CONVERT(VARCHAR, msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time), 120) ELSE '' END AS [LastRun], CASE WHEN h.run_duration IS NOT NULL THEN RIGHT('0' + CAST(h.run_duration / 10000 AS VARCHAR), 2) + ':' + RIGHT('0' + CAST((h.run_duration / 100) % 100 AS VARCHAR), 2) + ':' + RIGHT('0' + CAST(h.run_duration % 100 AS VARCHAR), 2) ELSE '' END AS [Duration], CASE WHEN ja.next_scheduled_run_date IS NOT NULL THEN CONVERT(VARCHAR, ja.next_scheduled_run_date, 120) ELSE '' END AS [NextRun], c.name AS [Category] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.syscategories c WITH (NOLOCK) ON j.category_id = c.category_id LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity ja WITH (NOLOCK) ON j.job_id = ja.job_id AND ja.session_id = (SELECT MAX(session_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity WITH (NOLOCK)) OUTER APPLY (SELECT TOP 1 run_status, run_date, run_time, run_duration FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory WITH (NOLOCK) WHERE job_id = j.job_id AND step_id = 0 ORDER BY run_date DESC, run_time DESC) h ORDER BY j.name

panels.title: Recent Failures — Detail
panels.description: Step-level detail for failed job executions in the last 24 hours.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job Name], h.step_id AS [Step], h.step_name AS [StepName], CONVERT(VARCHAR, msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time), 120) AS [RunTime], RIGHT('0' + CAST(h.run_duration / 10000 AS VARCHAR), 2) + ':' + RIGHT('0' + CAST((h.run_duration / 100) % 100 AS VARCHAR), 2) + ':' + RIGHT('0' + CAST(h.run_duration % 100 AS VARCHAR), 2) AS [Duration], h.sql_severity AS [Severity], REPLACE(REPLACE(h.message, CHAR(13), ' '), CHAR(10), ' ') AS [Message] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) ON h.job_id = j.job_id WHERE h.run_status = 0 AND msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time) >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE()) ORDER BY msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time) DESC

panels.title: Long Running Jobs (Now)
panels.description: Currently running jobs with their elapsed time.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job Name], ja.start_execution_date AS [Started], DATEDIFF(MINUTE, ja.start_execution_date, GETDATE()) AS [ElapsedMin], ISNULL(ja.last_executed_step_id, 0) AS [CurrentStep] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity ja WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) ON ja.job_id = j.job_id WHERE ja.session_id = (SELECT MAX(session_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity WITH (NOLOCK)) AND ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL ORDER BY ja.start_execution_date

panels.title: Job Schedules
panels.description: Active schedules for all enabled jobs.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job Name], s.name AS [Schedule], CASE s.freq_type WHEN 1 THEN 'Once' WHEN 4 THEN 'Daily' WHEN 8 THEN 'Weekly' WHEN 16 THEN 'Monthly' WHEN 32 THEN 'Monthly Relative' WHEN 64 THEN 'Agent Start' WHEN 128 THEN 'Idle' ELSE CAST(s.freq_type AS VARCHAR(10)) END AS [Frequency], CASE WHEN s.freq_type = 4 THEN 'Every ' + CAST(s.freq_interval AS VARCHAR) + ' day(s)' WHEN s.freq_type = 8 THEN 'Every ' + CAST(s.freq_recurrence_factor AS VARCHAR) + ' week(s)' WHEN s.freq_type = 16 THEN 'Day ' + CAST(s.freq_interval AS VARCHAR) + ' every ' + CAST(s.freq_recurrence_factor AS VARCHAR) + ' month(s)' ELSE '' END AS [Detail], CASE WHEN s.active_start_date > 0 THEN CONVERT(VARCHAR, CONVERT(DATETIME, CAST(s.active_start_date AS VARCHAR(8)), 112), 120) ELSE '' END AS [StartDate], CASE s.enabled WHEN 1 THEN 'Enabled' ELSE 'Disabled' END AS [ScheduleStatus] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobschedules js WITH (NOLOCK) ON j.job_id = js.job_id INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysschedules s WITH (NOLOCK) ON js.schedule_id = s.schedule_id WHERE j.enabled = 1 ORDER BY j.name, s.name
